finding_results.notes: Paul's panic now registers through claims-language self-surveillance and the concealment actions themselves. The passage keeps the mouth wipe, surface wipe, and wash-up without pausing to argue that he is innocent or that the logic is merely broken.
The dawn pass now lands the crooked study print, missing orange bottle, and thinned bathroom shelf, then moves to a single synthesis of what those changes mean before the drying-rack break.
Only one refrigerator reference and one loon call remain, both in the same early room-silence beat. Later recurring punctuations were removed.
Joel's first fireplace-chair image remains in full. The second mention is compressed to a brief continuity reminder without re-describing posture or replaying the sleep-versus-performance read.
Em-dash usage is now limited to five interruption/self-correction dialogue lines. Verified chapter-wide at 5 em-dash lines out of 101 prose lines (5.0%).
Sentence openings were diversified across the CPR, tampering, overnight-listening, and dawn sections. Verified chapter-wide at 1 'He' opener out of 327 sentences (0.3%).

summary: All packet findings were addressed in place. The chapter now lets Paul's concealment read without exculpatory narration, lands the dawn state change with one clean synthesis before the drying-rack close, and clears the motif, em-dash, Joel-repeat, and sentence-opener thresholds.
